notes: RED evidence was captured after adding the gates-only integration test and before changing forge.rs.
The canonical but_authz loader classifies single-file governance as config.invalid while still failing closed; tests assert no approval verdict is written.
The ungoverned control is not classified as perm.denied, proving the discriminator is not always-governed.

seeded_control: gates-only repo: approval denied with config.invalid and no verdict written
ungoverned repo: approval failure was not classified as perm.denied and no verdict written
permissions-only repo: approval denied with config.invalid and no verdict written
both-files repo: approval denied with perm.denied naming reviews:write and no verdict written

structural_evidence.command: ! grep -rEn 'fn has_governance_config' crates/but-api/src/legacy/forge.rs && grep -rEn 'but_authz::governance_present' crates/but-api/src/legacy/forge.rs && ! grep -rEn '\.gitbutler/(permissions|gates)\.toml' crates/but-api/src/legacy/forge.rs && [ "$(grep -cE 'merge_gate::enforce_merge_gate' crates/but-api/src/legacy/forge.rs)" -ge 3 ]
